description: Record rblock properties over time for plotting and post-processing.

python_sdk_alternative.workaround: Use itasca.command('rblock history ...') via command interface

keywords.name: displacement
keywords.description: Magnitude of the accumulated rigid block displacement vector as a result of cycling.

keywords.name: displacement-x
keywords.description: The x -component of accumulated displacement as a result of cycling.

keywords.name: displacement-y
keywords.description: The y -component of accumulated displacement as a result of cycling.

keywords.name: displacement-z
keywords.description: The z -component of accumulated displacement as a result of cycling.

keywords.name: euler-x
keywords.description: The x -euler angle (in degrees) of the current rigid block orientation. The orientation is updated only when orientation tracking has been enabled (see the model orientation-tracking command). See the euler keyword of the rblock attribute command.

keywords.name: euler-y
keywords.description: The y -euler angle (in degrees) of the current rigid block orientation. The orientation is updated only when orientation tracking has been enabled (see the model orientation-tracking command). See the euler keyword of the rblock attribute command.

keywords.name: euler-z
keywords.description: The z -euler angle (in degrees) of the current rigid block orientation. The orientation is updated only when orientation tracking has been enabled (see the model orientation-tracking command). See the euler keyword of the rblock attribute command.

keywords.name: force-contact
keywords.description: The magnitude of the force resulting from contacts.

keywords.name: force-contact-x
keywords.description: The x -component of the contact force.

keywords.name: force-contact-y
keywords.description: The y -component of the contact force.

keywords.name: force-contact-z
keywords.description: The z -component of the contact force.

keywords.name: force-unbalanced
keywords.description: The magnitude of the unbalanced force (sum of the applied, contact and gravitational forces).

keywords.name: force-unbalanced-x
keywords.description: The x -component of the unbalanced force.

keywords.name: force-unbalanced-y
keywords.description: The y -component of the unbalanced force.

keywords.name: force-unbalanced-z
keywords.description: The z -component of the unbalanced force.

keywords.name: moment-contact
keywords.description: {Value in 2D; Magnitude in 3D} of the sum of the contact moments.

keywords.description: The x -component of the contact moment.

keywords.name: moment-contact-y
keywords.description: The y -component of the contact moment.

keywords.name: moment-contact-z
keywords.description: The z -component of the contact moment.

keywords.name: moment-unbalanced
keywords.description: {Value in 2D; Magnitude in 3D} of the sum of the contact and applied moments.

keywords.name: moment-unbalanced-x
keywords.description: The x -component of the unbalanced moment.

keywords.name: moment-unbalanced-y
keywords.description: The y -component of the unbalanced moment.

keywords.name: moment-unbalanced-z
keywords.description: The z -component of the unbalanced moment.

keywords.name: position-x
keywords.description: The x -component of the location of rigid block centroid.

keywords.description: The y -component of the location of rigid block centroid.

keywords.name: position-z
keywords.description: The z -component of the location of rigid block centroid.

keywords.name: rotation
keywords.description: Current rigid block orientation. The orientation is updated only when orientation tracking has been enabled (see model orientation-tracking command).

keywords.name: magnitude
keywords.description: This keyword selects which scalar to retrieve from a vector type value, such as velocity or displacement. If the value type is not a vector, this setting is ignored. The available options are:

keywords.name: kwd:id
keywords.description: Record the history of rigid block with ID i .

keywords.name: kwd:index
keywords.description: For keywords that require it (most notably extra ), this specifies the index that should be used.

keywords.name: kwd:log
keywords.description: If on , the returned number is the base 10 log of the absolute value of the original value. The default is off .

keywords.name: kwd:position
keywords.description: Record the history of rigid block either containing or closest to position v .

keywords.name: zz
keywords.description: This keyword selects which scalar to retrieve from a symmetric tensor type value, such as stress or strain. If the value type is not a tensor, this setting is ignored. The available options are: Record the intermediate principal value of the tensor. Record the maximum (most positive) principal value of the tensor. Note that compressive stresses are negative . Record the mean value, defined as the trace of the tensor divided by { 2 in 2D; 3 in 3D}. For stresses this is most often referred to as the pressure. Record the minimum (most negative) principal value of the tensor. Note that compressive stresses are negative . Record the norm of the full tensor. This is defined as the sum of each tensor component squared. Record the octahedral measure of the tensor. Record the maximum shear value. Record the distance of the tensor value to the origin in principal space. Record the volumetric change, or trace of the tensor. Record the Von Mises measure. Record the xx -component of the tensor. Record the xy -component of the tensor. Record the xz -component of the tensor. Record the yy -component of the tensor. Record the yz -component of the tensor. Record the zz -component of the tensor.

keywords.name: vector
keywords.description: In certain cases the type (scalar, vector, or tensor) of the value cannot necessarily be determined ahead of time. Extra variables, for example, can hold values of all three types. This keyword allows one to specify which type it is assumed to be. If the original value type does not match, 0.0 is returned. Specify a scalar float type. Specify a tensor type. Specify a vector type.

keywords.description: This keyword selects which scalar to retrieve from a vector type value, such as velocity or displacement. If the value type is not a vector, this setting is ignored. The available options are: Record the \(x\) -component of the vector. Record the \(y\) -component of the vector. Record the \(z\) -component of the vector. Record the vector magnitude.

Record the mean value, defined as the trace of the tensor divided by { 2 in 2D; 3 in 3D}. For stresses this is most often referred to as the pressure. Record the minimum (most negative) principal value of the tensor. Note that compressive stresses are negative . Record the norm of the full tensor. This is defined as the sum of each tensor component squared. Record the octahedral measure of the tensor. Record the maximum shear value. Record the zz -component of the tensor.
